title: ANH19224 What is High Potassium (Hyperkalemia)
text_en: To help minimize your risk of hyperkalemia, it's important that you know your risk factors, potassium level. It's to avoid and medications you're taking. If you have questions about hyperkalemia or any medications you have been prescribed, talk to your doctor. It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect that changes normal blood flow through a baby's

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A healthy heart has four chambers, the right atrium and right ventricle, and the left atrium and left ventricle.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: After delivering oxygen to the body, oxygen poor blood flows into the right atrium. When the right atrium contracts, it loads blood into the relaxed right ventricle. Then the right ventricle contracts, sending blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The blood picks up oxygen in the lungs, then returns to the left atrium. The left atrium contracts, filling the relaxed left ventricle with blood. And the left ventricle contracts to pump and deliver oxygen-rich blood to the body through

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect made up of four problems with the heart and its blood vessels.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles. The hole allows oxygen-rich blood to mix with oxygen poor blood.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: An overriding aorta means the aorta is shifted slightly from its usual location on It sits right over the ventricular septal defect. This allows blood from both ventricles to flow through the aorta. As a result, some oxygen poor blood may flow to the body.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Pulmonary stenosis means that the main pulmonary artery is narrowed. And the pulmonary valve doesn't open all the way. As a result of the narrowing, less blood reaches the lungs.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than normal. This is thought to happen in response to blood flow through the ventricular septal defect, and from having to work harder to pump blood through the narrowed pulmonary artery, which results in increased blood pressure.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Together, these four problems may prevent enough oxygen from reaching the baby's body. This may result in episodes of a condition called cyanosis, or blue baby. In this condition, the baby's skin, lips, and fingernails may have a bluish tint.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: To find out more about tetralogy of follow, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The treatments for tetralogyofollow are surgical procedures to help fix the following four heart problems.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: An overriding aorta happens when the aorta is located directly over the ventricular septal defect. This means the aorta is connected to both the left and right ventricles instead of its normal attachment to only the left ventricle.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: In pulmonary stenosis, the main pulmonary artery is narrowed, and the pulmonary valve doesn't open all the way.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: And right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Tetralogyofollow is repaired with open heart surgery, either soon after birth or in Pricle procedures may include temporary and complete repairs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Your child may not be ready for a complete repair right away. In this case, a temporary repair, such as a shunt or a stent, can be placed until a complete repair can be done. Both repairs increase blood flow to the lungs and improve oxygen levels in the blood delivered to the body.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: During a shunt procedure, the surgeon will open the chest wall to place a tube called a shunt between a branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary artery. The shunt will provide another path for blood to get to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: During a stent procedure, a tube called a catheter will be inserted through a small incision into a blood vessel in the leg. The cardiologist will use the catheter to place a device called a stent inside the narrowed area of the pulmonary artery. This allows more blood to flow to the lungs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A complete repair is done when your child is strong enough for the procedure. During the procedure, the pulmonary stenosis defect may be repaired in the following ways.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If the main pulmonary artery is too small, it will be widened with a patch.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If the pulmonary valve is too small, it may be widened with a patch or replaced.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If extra heart muscle is blocking blood flow below the pulmonary valve, the surgeon will remove the muscle tissue.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: These repairs will improve the flow of oxygen poor blood from the right ventricle to the lungs. As a result, the blood will get enough oxygen for the body's needs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: To fix the ventricular septal defect, the surgeon will cover the hole with a patch. The patch will prevent the mixing of oxygen poor blood with oxygen rich blood. As a result, only oxygen rich blood will flow out of the aorta to the body from the left

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Over time, fixing the pulmonary stenosis and closing the ventricular septal defect will also fix the right ventricular hypertrophy. Since the right ventricle won't have to work as hard to pump blood, its wall will return to normal thickness.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If your child still has a shunt from the temporary repair, it will either be removed or clipped shut at the end of this procedure. Similarly, if your child had a stent placed, it will also be removed.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: To find out more about treatment for tetralogy of follow, talk to your health care provider.
